title: Nerv and Eva04 canadian or us
display_title: Nerv and Eva04 canadian or us
wikitext:
[]: Nerv&Eva04 canadian or u.s ?             Quote Post by Deyno » Tue Oct 25, 2011 11:08 am Well this is simple ... I checked the geography and concluded that Eva04 & Nerv are in Canada, as shown in the next picture: rebuild 2.22 min53 saludos/greetings

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 25, 2011 1:25 pm Okaaaaay? Well, NERV-02 was in like...Nevada. Nowhere near Canada.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Oct 25, 2011 1:31 pm Many canon sources (in addition to the show itself) explicitly state that Nerv 2nd Branch is located in the United States. Most likely, someone at Gainax fails at geography.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Oct 25, 2011 1:36 pm Well, episode 17 does: . Eva-04 should have been at the second branch because the dialog says that it and Eva-04 "Vanished". Edit: unless... This is Rebuild related?

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Tue Oct 25, 2011 2:11 pm Trajan wrote: Most likely, someone at Gainax fails at geography.

[]:             Quote Post by Deyno » Tue Oct 25, 2011 2:32 pm I know that in NGE NERV # 2 is in Nevada, I refer to rebuild sorry can a MOD to move this thread to the section "Rebuild of Evangelion" [/b]

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Oct 25, 2011 3:29 pm This seems like a rather trivial detail to change. I'm not sure if I buy into it. Is this the only evidence we have to suggest this?

[]:             Quote Post by Deyno » Tue Oct 25, 2011 5:51 pm the Nerv quarters #1 (EEUU) and eva03 are nearby Monument Valley to Utah / Arizona

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Wed Oct 26, 2011 12:25 am semi-related question: How would this affect people through out the country, anyway? I live on the east coast, so I'd like to know if I'd get killed, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Wed Oct 26, 2011 12:31 am Seen wrote: semi-related question: How would this affect people through out the country, anyway? I live on the east coast, so I'd like to know if I'd get killed, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Oct 26, 2011 2:01 am Tried to get a screenshot of the same perspective from Google Earth: http://www.tesuji.org/images/googleearth_eva04_explosion.png Compare that to the high res screenshot from 2.0: http://www.tesuji.org/images/evangelion_2.0_earth_eva04_explosion.png A lot of the terrain match up pretty closely, you can see the matching mountain ranges, rivers, and lakes. This puts the source of the explosion squarely in British Columbia, kind of in the middle of nowhere, somewhere south of "Upper Arrow Lake" and west of "Kootenay Lake". Maybe somewhere near "Mount Bor" or "Gladsheim Peak"?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Oct 26, 2011 2:03 am May have something to do with 2nd Impact.... :p

[]: BLAH! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Oct 26, 2011 3:58 am Deyno wrote: the Nerv quarters #1 (EEUU) and eva03 are nearby Monument Valley to Utah / Arizona

[]:             Quote Post by Deyno » Wed Oct 26, 2011 8:23 am in NGE U.S.: Masachuset & eva03 + Nevada & eva04 Germany: Hamburg and Berlin with Eva02 Rebuild instead is more distributed USA: Utah / Arizona, eva03 Canada: British Columbia, Eva04 Germany: Eva02 Russia: Eva05 _____________________ Seen wrote: semi-related question: How would this affect people through out the country, anyway? I live on the east coast, so I'd like to know if I'd get killed, too.

[]:             Quote Post by AdmiralDerp » Sun Nov 13, 2011 7:57 pm Woah. I live right there, in Castlegar BC. It definitely looks very close to here on the map. Nice find.

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Sun Nov 13, 2011 9:24 pm Deyno wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 03, 2011 1:17 pm Nice work, Deyno and Ornette.

[]:             Quote Post by Dirty Mick » Wed Dec 07, 2011 8:15 pm Deyno wrote: the Nerv quarters #1 (EEUU) and eva03 are nearby Monument Valley to Utah / Arizona

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 07, 2011 8:20 pm Dirty Mick wrote: I don't remember seeing that in the movie

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Sun Dec 18, 2011 2:45 am Silly Canadians. Of course maple syrup isn't a substitute for LCL. Or perhaps a herd of wild moose broke through the perimeter and shattered Unit-04's core. Or. OR! Half the programming was in English and the other half was in French. Yeah, I'm pretty sure its about time the Quebecois blew up the world somehow.
